channel.title: Comments for page "Pattern Breaker"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Pattern Breaker"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is how you cross-reference.

To be fair, I am slightly breaking the first rule of cross-referring here - this whole tale (and Our Mutual Guide) is an exercise in "my anomaly is *way* more dangerous than these other anomalies". Still, as a way to escalate the stakes without revealing the precise nature of the threat, it's an invaluable shortcut!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Calling it now – the tone is actually the Secret Chord.

It's a good guess, Aiden, and we've been looking at SCP-1012 in the context of the canon. But it's not what happens when you play On Mount Golgotha. We're thinking much bigger than that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Not this child - not this child," said Jayden, shaking his head and rocking in place. "If this child grows up, it will destroy all of the other children. All of their voices, lost. Their beauty gone. A world without music. You cannot allow this child to grow. My friend told me, and my friend was afraid."

Dang! This is how you cross-reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When the final note plays and St. Alagadda returns, everything will be pressed together - scales and scales, every note in every register becoming a single tone heard forever, without limit.

Calling it now – the tone is actually the Secret Chord.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent work as always. Your tales continue to feature some of the strongest characters and most satisfying character interactions that I've had the pleasure to read on this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been following this one for a while, but I had only gotten to see as far as Emma presenting her music to ProfetiX.

And SCP-2992's full reaction to what SCP-012 represents… That's beautiful in its horror. Incredible work. And then it's backed up by the increasingly unhinged Emma.

I love your work, psul. I've got to keep adding to it.

It's not finished, yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Another On Mount Golgotha tale - now the third addition to the canon in the past three weeks.

This story takes us back to Emma Stark, who is trying to come to terms with the events of A Convincing Lyre and the subsequent theft of SCP-012. And if many of the Foundation contacts she would usually turn to are unavailable, it might be time to break that pattern and speak to another anomaly about what they know. Although perhaps a broken pattern is just a different kind of pattern?

I am indebted to those who reviewed and provided feedback - Tuomey, Hercules Rockefeller, Steeltitan and WrongJohnSilver. And particularly to SoullessSingularity, who not only blessed the use of their SCP-2992, but gave some excellent feedback on how to write for that character. Thank you very much!
